Layout.description: A simple tool for syncing audiobooks and ebooks
Layout.title: Storyteller

LoginPage.title: Login
LoginPage.somethingWentWrongPleaseTryAgain: Something went wrong. Please try again.

SettingsPage.title: Settings
SettingsPage.lockedSettings: Some settings are managed via the configuration file and cannot be changed here.
SettingsPage.lockedSettingTooltip: This setting is locked because it is managed via the settings configuration file.

library.title: Library Settings
library.description: Configure your library name and web URL. These are used in for instance emails and the OPDS feed.

autoImport.title: Auto-Import
autoImport.description: Configure automatic book import from a directory
autoImport.explanation: Storyteller can be configured to automatically import book files from a specific directory. When enabled, Storyteller will set up a filesystem watcher for the directory and automatically import any new book files it finds.

readaloudLocation.title: Readaloud Location
readaloudLocation.description: Configure where new readaloud files are saved when the input files were not uploaded through the web client

audio.title: Audio Settings
audio.maxTrackLengthDescription: Audio tracks longer than this will be split before transcribing. This can help reduce memory usage during transcription.

transcription.title: Transcription Settings
transcription.description: Configure how Storyteller transcribes audiobook narration to text. By default, transcription runs locally using whisper.cpp. You can also use paid third-party services.
transcription.whisperBuildDeprecated: cuBLAS 11.8 is no longer supported. Please update to version 12 or 13.
transcription.whisperModelDescription: The default (tiny) is sufficient for most English books. For books with many uncommon words, or in languages other than English, you may need larger models like small or medium.
transcription.whisperThreadsDescription: Change the parallelization level of the Whisper model. Can result in a massive speed increase when doing GPU-accelerated transcription.
transcription.whisperThreadsNote: It is not recommended to set both this value and the "Number of audio tracks to transcribe in parallel" value to a value greater than 1. Choose one or the other.
transcription.whisperThreadsWarning: Setting above 1 may reduce transcription accuracy but increases speed by splitting audio into chunks processed in parallel. Do not report bugs if you set this to a value greater than 1 and are not able to get a consistent Readaloud, only if you cannot get a consistent Readaloud even with a value of 1.
transcription.whisperCpuFallbackDescription: If you have a GPU variant installed but want to fall back to CPU transcription, select a CPU variant here.
transcription.whisperServerUrlDescription: e.g. http://192.168.1.19:8080 for the local whisper.cpp server.
transcription.whisperServerApiKeyDescription: Only necessary if your server requires an API key.
transcription.whisperServerDescription: Use a remote, self-hosted whisper.cpp server for transcription. Useful if you have a powerful machine to offload transcription to.
transcription.bucketNameDescription: Amazon Transcribe requires files to be uploaded to an S3 bucket before starting the transcribe job. This is the bucket Storyteller will upload files to.
transcription.baseUrlDescription: You can use a custom base URL to point at a OpenAI-compatible service like a self-hosted speaches instance or a remote whisper.cpp HTTP server.
transcription.modelNameDescription: e.g. Systran/faster-distil-whisper-large-v3 for faster-whisper-server, or whisper-1 for OpenAI Cloud.
transcription.deepgramModelDescription: Can be any model the server supports, like nova-3, nova-2, nova, enhanced, base or whisper. Defaults to nova-3.

parallelization.title: Parallelization Settings
parallelization.description: Audio transcoding is single-threaded, and Whisper has diminishing returns on multi-core processing for a single file. Since Storyteller splits input audio into multiple tracks, it can run transcoding and transcription in parallel.
parallelization.parallelTranscodesDescription: Transcoding one track will use one CPU core
parallelization.parallelTranscribesDescription: Transcribing one track will use up to 4 CPU cores (when using CPU-based transcription)
parallelization.parallelWhisperBuildDescription: Whenever the Storyteller container is recreated (e.g. after an update), it will rebuild whisper.cpp. This process can be sped up by dedicating multiple CPU cores.

sso.title: Authentication Providers
sso.description: Configure external authentication providers for single sign-on
sso.name: Name *
sso.allowRegistrationDescription: Automatically create accounts for new users from this provider.
sso.groupPermissionsDescription: Map OIDC groups to permissions. If specified, users not in any listed group will be denied access.
sso.disablePasswordLoginDescriptionEnabled: Only allow login via configured authentication providers. Most OPDS clients do not support OAuth.
sso.disablePasswordLoginDescriptionDisabled: Requires an auth provider with a group that can change server settings.

upload.title: Upload Settings
upload.description: Configure upload chunk size for large file uploads
upload.overriddenWarning: Your max chunk size is overridden via the environment variable STORYTELLER_MAX_UPLOAD_CHUNK_SIZE. Change that environment variable to change the value, or unset it to configure the value here.
upload.enableMaxChunkSizeDescription: Only enable this if you're running into maximum request size issues with your reverse proxy or hosting provider.
upload.maxChunkSizeDescription: Default is 100MB, which is Cloudflare's maximum request size.

email.title: Email Settings
email.description: Configure SMTP settings for sending emails (invitations, password resets, etc.)
email.forceTlsDescription: Disabling this option does not disable TLS. By default, Storyteller will use STARTTLS to negotiate a TLS connection. If you have this option enabled and see an error about an incorrect TLS version, try disabling it.

opds.title: OPDS Settings
opds.description: Configure the OPDS feed for your library. OPDS is a standard for publishing e-book catalogs.
opds.enabledDescription: Enable the OPDS feed for your library. This allows compatible e-book readers to browse your library and download books. You can access the OPDS feed at {url}.
opds.pageSizeDescription: The number of books per page in the OPDS feed. Default is 25.
